access/faccessat2 errno matrix and AT_EMPTY_PATH check
statx mask/size/type/nlink coherence and ENOENT
close_range close vs CLOSE_RANGE_CLOEXEC and EINVAL
prctl PR_SET/GET_NAME and TIMERSLACK round-trip plus EINVAL
getrandom zero-length, GRND_NONBLOCK fill/entropy and EINVAL
sysinfo/uname/times coherent Linux facts (arch-neutral)
sendfile and splice zero-copy byte counts and content
umask affects create mode and returns prior mask
self-referential symlink terminates path resolution with ELOOP (symlink-depth cap)
clock_gettime with a bad user pointer returns EFAULT instead of faulting the guest
open past the soft RLIMIT_NOFILE returns EMFILE
write past RLIMIT_FSIZE raises SIGXFSZ
pipe2 rejects an invalid flag bit with EINVAL
rt_sigaction validates sigsetsize (EINVAL for size != 8)
faccessat rejects an invalid mode bit with EINVAL
getrandom rejects an invalid flag bit with EINVAL
prctl PR_SET/GET_TIMERSLACK round-trips
pwritev2 RWF_APPEND writes at EOF (offset ignored); preadv2 positioned read leaves file offset unchanged
sendfile non-NULL offset advances *off but not the input file position; count clamps at EOF
copy_file_range same-file overlapping ranges fail EINVAL; non-overlap and cross-file clamp advance offsets
F_GETPIPE_SZ/F_SETPIPE_SZ round-trip and dup3 flag semantics (same-fd EINVAL, O_CLOEXEC, bad flag)
writev iovcnt validation: above IOV_MAX or negative -> EINVAL, exactly IOV_MAX legal
splice file->pipe explicit off_in advances *off_in but leaves the input file position unchanged
ftruncate/truncate/fallocate and unlinked write past RLIMIT_FSIZE raise SIGXFSZ
inotify fd must reserve a real host descriptor so a later absolute-path open cannot reuse and clobber the watch number (read/poll EBADF)
an armed inotify watch is epollable: epoll_ctl ADD succeeds and epoll_wait returns EPOLLIN with round-tripped epoll_data when an event queues; select/poll/epoll agree; EPOLL_CTL_DEL cleans up
sysinfo(2) totalram==/proc/meminfo MemTotal and uptime==/proc/uptime; freeram<=totalram, procs>0 (old sysinfo hardcoded 8 GiB totalram + constant 3600 uptime)
getrusage ru_maxrss high-water grows + RUSAGE_CHILDREN accrues, times monotonic, uname machine==guest arch, sysconf phys pages*pagesize==MemTotal
seccomp(2) SECCOMP_GET_ACTION_AVAIL + SECCOMP_GET_NOTIF_SIZES capability probes (libseccomp/runc) plus SO_ATTACH_FILTER classic-BPF attach; all match native

fcntl command matrix: F_GETFL/SETFL masking (O_NONBLOCK/O_APPEND/O_DIRECT round-trip, accmode+creation+O_SYNC not changeable), FD_CLOEXEC round-trip + O_CLOEXEC-at-open on device nodes, F_DUPFD(_CLOEXEC) floor+shared-offset, F_GETOWN/SETOWN, F_SETSIG/GETSIG + O_ASYNC custom-signal delivery, F_SETLEASE EAGAIN, F_NOTIFY

modern process/fd startup surface: dup3 old==new EINVAL + O_CLOEXEC, close_range CLOEXEC/close/lo>hi/badflag, close EBADF edges, name_to_handle_at succeeds while open_by_handle_at is EPERM (was ENOSYS), execveat AT_EMPTY_PATH fexecve re-exec, and rseq/getcpu per-cpu fallback stays self-consistent (getcpu==sched_getcpu, in range)

F_ADD_SEALS/F_GET_SEALS forward to the host fd for a non-memfd: a tmpfs file reports its real F_SEAL_SEAL state (not blanket EINVAL); real memfd seal round-trip + F_SEAL_WRITE-while-mapped EBUSY + write/shrink EPERM

getrandom(2) syscall security contract: full-buffer fill + count, two draws differ, gross-failure detection (distinct-byte count, no short period, high+low bytes), n=0->0, bad buffer EFAULT, unknown flag EINVAL, GRND_RANDOM|GRND_INSECURE EINVAL, AT_RANDOM non-zero; raw SYS_getrandom so the engine handler is the oracle on both sides
getentropy(3) contract: exact-fill 256/32, two draws differ, not-all-equal, n>256 EIO; locks the host-RNG plumbing at the libc layer
adversarial: socketpair/getsockname/readlinkat into a straddling/unmapped result buffer return EFAULT instead of faulting the engine
adversarial: getpeername/accept/rt_sigprocmask(oldset+set,incl x86 W4F inline)/sched_getattr into a straddling/unmapped result buffer return EFAULT instead of faulting the engine
adversarial: listxattr/recvmmsg/process_vm_readv+writev (result scattered through an engine scratch buffer) into a straddling/unmapped guest buffer return an error instead of faulting the engine
